step.title: Step 18
step.actionDisplay: RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT

sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 6 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 54, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 53 of about 61 target actions so far.

Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Extends trail 2 cols right, but stops at col 40 (VERIFIED step 48)', 'LEFT': 'Shrinks trail 2 cols from right (VERIFIED step 26)', 'UP': 'Moves player up 6 rows (VERIFIED step 22)', 'DOWN': 'Moves player down 6 rows (VERIFIED step 37)', 'ACTION7': 'Locks in target connection when trail fully overlaps target (VERIFIED for red at step 28, failed for green at step 48)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'player': 'Pink (M) 6x6 square, 18 pixels', 'targets': 'red (25-28,18-21, completed), blue (31-34,42-45), green/N (37-40,42-45)', 'trail': 'Alternating gray/white 2x2 segments, 2 rows at player middle (rows 38-39 when player at 36-41)'}
- HUD map: {'hud_bar': 'Rows 53-63, progress bar + target order (pink, red, green, blue)'}
- Win pattern: Connect trail to each target in order (red, green, blue) and press ACTION7
Current level:
- World model: Player at rows 36-41, trail at rows 38-39. Trail ends at col 40, green at cols 42-45. Trail won't extend beyond col 40. ACTION7 failed because trail doesn't reach green.
- Goal model: Connect trail to green, lock with ACTION7, then do blue. Trail must fully overlap target's columns.
- Recent findings: ['Trail stops at col 40 regardless of RIGHT presses', 'ACTION7 requires trail to overlap target columns, not just be adjacent']
- Open questions: ['How to make trail reach green at cols 42-45? Maybe need to move green or change player position?']
- Plan: Try moving player to different rows to see if trail can extend further, or try MOUSE on green to select it first
- Strategy log: ['Tried extending trail with RIGHT: stops at col 40 (step 48)']
- Failed probes: ['MOUSE on green: no change (step 24)', 'ACTION7 without trail overlap: no change (step 48)', 'RIGHT beyond col 40: no extension (step 48)']

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The trail won't extend beyond col 40, and green is at cols 42-45. RIGHT doesn't extend the trail further. ACTION7 doesn't work without full overlap.
Let me reconsider the mechanics. For red, the player was at rows 24-29, and the trail was at rows 26-27. The trail extended to col 40, and red was at cols 36-39. So the trail overlapped with red's columns (36-39 is within the trail's range).
For green, the player is at rows 36-41, and the trail is at rows 38-39. The trail ends at col 40, and green is at cols 42-45. The trail doesn't overlap with green's columns.
Wait, maybe I need to look at this differently. The trail extends from the player's column (col 13) to the right. For red, the trail extended from col 13 to col 40, and red was at cols 36-39. So the trail covered red's columns.
For green, the trail also extends from col 13 to col 40, but green is at cols 42-45. The trail doesn't cover green's columns.
Hmm, but the trail stops at col 40. Maybe the trail can only extend so far, and I need to move the player closer to green? But the player is already at the bottom row (rows 36-41), and green is at rows 37-40. The player's horizontal position is at col 13, and green is at cols 42-45.
Wait, maybe I need to move the player horizontally? But the valid actions are UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7. LEFT and RIGHT extend/shrink the trail, they don't move the player horizontally.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the trail doesn't need to physically reach the target. Maybe ACTION7 works differently. Let me look at the red connection again: when I pressed ACTION7 for red, the trail was at rows 26-27, and red was at rows 25-28. The trail was within red's rows, and the trail extended to col 40, which was beyond red's cols (36-39). So the trail covered red's columns.
For green, the trail is at rows 38-39, and green is at rows 37-40. The trail is within green's rows. But the trail ends at col 40, and green is at cols 42-45. The trail doesn't cover green's columns.
Maybe I need to move the player so that the trail is at a different horizontal position? But the player can only move UP and DOWN, not LEFT and RIGHT.
Wait, let me look at the image again. The player is at the left side (col 13), and the trail extends to the right. The targets are on the right side. For red, the trail extended far enough to cover red. For green, the trail doesn't extend far enough.
Maybe the issue is that the trail can only extend a certain distance, and I need to be closer to the target horizontally. But the player can't move horizontally...
Unless... maybe I need to use MOUSE to move the player? Or maybe there's a different mechanic I'm missing.
Let me try pressing ACTION7 again, but this time check if anything changed in the segmentation (maybe the trail color changed or something).
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the red connection. When I pressed ACTION7 for red, what happened? The trail was at rows 26-27, and red was at rows 25-28. The trail covered red's columns (36-39). After ACTION7, red was "completed" (maybe it changed color or disappeared?).
Let me check the current state of red in the segmentation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state of all targets and trail
seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Find all colored objects (not background, not trail, not player)
for n in seg['nodes']:
    r_min = min(b[0] for b in n['boundary'])
    r_max = max(b[0] for b in n['boundary'])
    c_min = min(b[1] for b in n['boundary'])
    c_max = max(b[1] for b in n['boundary'])
    if n['color'] in ('R', 'b', 'N', 'M'):
        if n['pixels'] > 5:  # Not HUD elements
            print(f"{n['color']}: rows {r_min}-{r_max}, cols {c_min}-{c_max}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check if red changed (completed state)
red_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R' and n['pixels'] > 5]
print(f"Red nodes: {len(red_nodes)}")
for n in red_nodes:
    r_min = min(b[0] for b in n['boundary'])
    c_min = min(b[1] for b in n['boundary'])
    print(f"  Red: rows {r_min}-{max(b[0] for b in n['boundary'])}, cols {c_min}-{max(b[1] for b in n['boundary'])}")

# Check trail segments near green
trail_near_green = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] in ('w', 'g') and n['pixels'] == 4]
trail_max_col = 0
for n in trail_near_green:
    c_max = max(b[1] for b in n['boundary'])
    if c_max > trail_max_col:
        trail_max_col = c_max
print(f"Trail max col: {trail_max_col}")

# Check if green is adjacent to any trail node
green_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and min(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) < 50]
for gn in green_nodes:
    green_id = gn['id']
    adj = [pair for pair in seg['adjacency_list'] if green_id in pair]
    adj_ids = [pair[0] if pair[1] == green_id else pair[1] for pair in adj]
    adj_colors = [seg['nodes'][aid]['color'] for aid in adj_ids]
    print(f"Green adj colors: {adj_colors}")

# Maybe try MOUSE on the gap between trail and green?
# Or try ACTION7 with player at different position?
# Let's try moving player UP to see if trail changes
action(['UP'])
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
trail_max_col2 = 0
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('w', 'g') and n['pixels'] == 4:
        c_max = max(b[1] for b in n['boundary'])
        if c_max > trail_max_col2:
            trail_max_col2 = c_max
print(f"Trail max col after UP: {trail_max_col2}")

# Move back DOWN
action(['DOWN'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Extends trail 2 cols right, stops at col 40", "LEFT": "Shrinks trail 2 cols from right", "UP": "Moves player up 6 rows", "DOWN": "Moves player down 6 rows", "ACTION7": "Locks in target connection when trail overlaps target"}, "object_taxonomy": {"player": "Pink (M) 6x6 square", "targets": "red (completed), blue, green/N", "trail": "Alternating gray/white 2x2 segments"}, "hud_map": {"hud_bar": "Rows 53-63"}, "win_pattern": "Connect trail to targets in order, press ACTION7", "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Trail stops at col 40, green at cols 42-45. ACTION7 fails. Need to figure out how to connect.", "goal_model": "Connect trail to green, lock with ACTION7", "recent_findings": ["Trail won't extend beyond col 40", "ACTION7 requires trail to overlap target"], "open_questions": ["How to connect trail to green? Maybe need different player position or mechanic?"], "current_plan": "Check if moving player changes trail behavior, or try different approach", "strategy_log": ["Tried RIGHT: trail stops at col 40", "Tried ACTION7: failed"], "failed_probes": ["MOUSE on green", "ACTION7 without overlap", "RIGHT beyond col 40"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
